source: Multiple secondary sources (news, vendor announcements, city council reports)

program_details.distinction: First and most mature DFR program in the United States

technology_stack.software: Skydio Autonomy engine for autonomous flight

operational_metrics.coverage: Expanded from downtown to broader city coverage

program_history.2019: Initial pilot program and FAA Part 107 waiver
program_history.2020: Formal program launch as first US DFR program
program_history.2021: Expansion of coverage area and operational hours
program_history.2022: Continued scaling, Power BI public dashboard launched
program_history.2023: Exceeded 10,000 total missions, fleet modernization to Skydio X10
program_history.2024_2025: Continued operations, program model replicated by other cities

data_quality_notes: Chula Vista website URL restructured; program page may have moved. Power BI dashboard was publicly accessible but is JS-rendered. Key metrics well-established across multiple secondary sources. FOIA request recommended for granular deployment data. This is the flagship DFR program and most frequently cited in DFR literature.

cog_sci_relevance.response_time: Endsley SA — faster aerial arrival does not guarantee better situational comprehension
cog_sci_relevance.mission_volume: Mackworth vigilance — high mission frequency tests sustained attention limits
cog_sci_relevance.autonomous_flight: Bargh automaticity — operator trust in autonomous systems may produce complacency
cog_sci_relevance.video_streaming: Head & Helton change blindness — real-time video monitoring susceptible to attention failures
